texto: Tethered to the universe by tendrils of history, with threads of continuity descending to God knows where, I see that I'm more than the dust I'll become." This quote is from my novel, "Whispers from St. Mary's Well." Many readers have said that, like the fictional narrator of the story, Carrie Rose Stillwell, they felt a deep connection to the universe through past, present, and future experiences, after reading the story of a child who communicates with future generations.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Carol Kenny: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “Tethered to the universe by tendrils of history, with threads of continuity descending to God kno…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, family-saga, historical-fiction pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e historical-fiction.

This quote is from my novel, "Whispers from St. Mary's Well." Many readers have said that, like the fictional narrator of the story, Carrie Rose Stillwell, they felt a deep connection to the universe through past, present, and future experiences, after reading the story of a child who communicates with future generations.
